name: Ermita de la Virgen de la Encina por el camino a Majada Vieja — circular desde Baños de la Encina
region_intro: Jogging around Baños De La Encina offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, historical sites, and natural trails. The region features paths that wind past ancient castles and hermitages, often providing scenic viewpoints. Many routes traverse areas with holm oaks and Mediterranean scrub, offering varied terrain for runners. Best jogging routes around Baños De La Encina The most popular jogging route is Ermita de la Virgen de la Encina por el camino a Majada Vieja — circular desde Baños de la Encina, a 9.2 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular path from Baños de la Encina. Another top favourite among local runners is Castillo de Burgalimar y fortín de Migaldías - circular trailrunning por el sendero del Bronce, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route is a circular trail running experience past historical fortifications. Local runners also love the Baños de la Encina loop from Baños de la Encina, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through the local surroundings, often completed in about 29 minutes. Jogging around Baños De La Encina is defined by historical landmarks, varied elevation changes, and natural trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Baños De La Encina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Baños De La Encina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Baños De La Encina?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Baños De La Encina, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 25 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Baños De La Encina?
A: Yes, Baños De La Encina offers several easy jogging routes suitable for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304406616/ascent-to-mirador-pozo-nuevo-circular-from-el-centenillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Mirador Pozo Nuevo — circular from El Centenillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 3 km long and features minimal elevation gain. Another accessible choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de la Encina loop from Baños de la Encina&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.5 km trail that local runners enjoy.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Baños De La Encina area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running trails in Baños De La Encina are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304387885/ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-encina-por-el-camino-a-majada-vieja-circular-desde-banos-de-la-encina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Virgen de la Encina por el camino a Majada Vieja — circular desde Baños de la Encina&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.9 km loop. Another excellent circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989311533/castillo-de-burgalimar-y-fortin-de-migaldias-circular-trailrunning-por-el-sendero-del-bronce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar y fortín de Migaldías - circular trailrunning por el sendero del Bronce&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10 km long and passes historical fortifications.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the running trails?
A: Yes, the routes often feature scenic viewpoints and natural attractions. You can find stunning views of the Rumblar Reservoir from several points, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing sight. For panoramic vistas, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1278398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pastores Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Baños De La Encina?
A: The running trails in Baños De La Encina are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 5.0 stars from over 16 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, historical landmarks, and the natural beauty of the holm oaks and Mediterranean scrub along the paths.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while running?
A: Baños De La Encina is rich in history, and many running routes pass by significant landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989311533/castillo-de-burgalimar-y-fortin-de-migaldias-circular-trailrunning-por-el-sendero-del-bronce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar y fortín de Migaldías - circular trailrunning por el sendero del Bronce&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you past historical fortifications. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3509072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalosa Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, which is near some of the routes around the Rumblar Reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails?
A: The running trails in Baños De La Encina feature varied terrain. You'll encounter rolling hills, natural dirt paths, and sections through Mediterranean scrub and holm oak forests. The landscape is characterized by its historical elements and natural beauty, offering a mix of surfaces for an engaging run.

Q: Are there any routes that go near water bodies like lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several running routes offer views of the Rumblar Reservoir. You can find trails that provide glimpses of the water, such as those leading to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; or various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6698841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Baños De La Encina?
A: The longest route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304387885/ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-encina-por-el-camino-a-majada-vieja-circular-desde-banos-de-la-encina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Virgen de la Encina por el camino a Majada Vieja — circular desde Baños de la Encina&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 14.9 km (9.2 miles) and is typically completed in about 1 hour and 39 minutes.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly good for trail running?
A: For trail running enthusiasts, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989311533/castillo-de-burgalimar-y-fortin-de-migaldias-circular-trailrunning-por-el-sendero-del-bronce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar y fortín de Migaldías - circular trailrunning por el sendero del Bronce&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 10 km moderate route offers a true trail running experience through varied terrain and past historical sites.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on the running routes?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across the routes. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304406616/ascent-to-mirador-pozo-nuevo-circular-from-el-centenillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Mirador Pozo Nuevo&lt;/a&gt; have minimal gain, around 40-45 meters. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304387885/ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-encina-por-el-camino-a-majada-vieja-circular-desde-banos-de-la-encina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Virgen de la Encina&lt;/a&gt;, can feature around 225 meters of ascent, providing a good workout.
tips: Also known as Burgalimar Castle, it is a magnificent example of Andalusian military architecture from the Caliphate period, as its structure has hardly suffered any modifications or damage.

Romanesque sanctuary dating from the 13th century that was reformed in the 17th century.
Built in ashlar stone with a barrel vault, high choir, screen, and seven side altars.

waypoints.name: Castle of Baños de la Encina (Burgalimar Castle)
Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Encina
